pages.text:
§b§lFISHERMAN'S
    ANECDOTE§r

§7§oMusings from an old
salt who spent his
years listening to
the whispers of
the tide...§r

§8— Old Seadog

§b§lOf Rain and Dreams§r

§7§oThey say when the
skies weep upon the
waters, the fish
swim closer to the
surface... and perhaps
your dreams swim
closer too.§r

§8Cast your line when
the clouds gather.

§b§lThe Enchanted Rod§r

§7§oA wise angler once
told me: 'Bless your
rod with the sea's
own fortune, and the
deep shall reward
your patience.'

The stronger the
blessing, the more
vivid your dreams.§r

§b§lOf Worthy Dreamers§r

§7§oNot all treasures
reveal themselves to
the uninitiated.

The rarest catches
demand a dreamer who
has proven their
worth... their spirit
must be bright enough
to attract them.§r

§b§lBottled Dreams§r

§7§oSometimes the sea
gifts you more than
fish. Strange brews
wash up in the crates
she offers...

Drink deep, and your
dreams may shine
brighter for a time.§r

§b§lThe Ocean's Bounty§r

§7§oEach body of water
holds different
secrets. The cold
seas yield frost,
the deep yields void.

Travel far, cast wide,
and let the waters
teach you their
individual songs.§r

§b§lThe Shroud of Tides§r

§7§oLegends speak of
armor blessed by
Poseidon himself...

Those who gather all
four pieces find
themselves at home
beneath the waves.

§8Tight lines, friend.
May your dreams
find you.§r
